name: Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (2019)
summary: Thailand's comprehensive personal data protection law, enacted in 2019 with full enforcement from 1 June 2022. It governs the collection, use, and disclosure of personal data by data controllers and processors, with provisions closely modeled on the EU GDPR including lawful bases for processing and data subject rights.
keyRequirements: Obtain consent for collection, use, or disclosure of personal data unless a lawful basis applies
Notify data subjects about purposes of data collection before or at the time of collection
Implement appropriate security measures to prevent unauthorized access or disclosure
Appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO) in specified circumstances
Data subject rights including access, rectification, erasure, portability, and objection
Mandatory data breach notification to the PDPC within 72 hours
Restrictions on cross-border data transfers requiring adequate protection
penalties: Administrative fines up to THB 5 million (~USD 140,000). Criminal penalties up to THB 1 million and/or up to 1 year imprisonment for certain violations. Civil liability for actual damages plus punitive damages up to 2x actual damages. Enforced by the Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC).
